body: The MQTT broker auth store may not propagate after reprovision; verify with a direct paho-mqtt connect probe before changing route code. Look for CONNACK rc=0 in the response.
petro_backend overrides the DB MQTT port 1883 to 443 over WSS at /api/v1/mqtt-ws. Match this when the route fails.
Do NOT add Redis. We removed Redis 2026-03 in favor of in-process caching. Any architected prompt should explicitly warn against re-introducing it.
Secret-redaction safeguards live in daemon/mnemo/safeguards.py. Output that may leave the daemon (paste-bound prompts) must pass through redact_text first.

On Windows, os.kill(pid, 0) lies about live processes -- it can return False even when a process is bound to a port. v5.6.0's _listener_pid_for_port uses psutil to query the OS for the actual listener, which is the only reliable source of truth.

Two-source-of-truth lifecycle: the pid file is a cache; the OS port-binding is truth. When they disagree, the listener wins. DaemonStatus.orphaned=True flags this; stop() should target the listener pid (recovers orphans automatically).

MCP-spawned subprocesses must warm cold-load services during the handshake, not lazily on first tool call. Initialize is long or untimed; tool calls are short. v5.5.1's prepare_stdio_server eager-loads the sentence-transformer so the first mnemo_query doesn't time out at ~15s.
BASE-flagged nodes bypass project isolation in retrieval. Default workspace is isolated to current project; nodes flagged BASE (reference_mnemo_pipelines, reference_mnemo_v3) are universal.

Hybrid Graph-RAG retrieval: vector similarity (sqlite-vec, MiniLM 384-dim) + graph adjacency boost + freshness decay. Default budget cap 800 tokens. All hits cite [mnemo:<node_id>] so Claude can cite back.
MMR re-rank: feedback signals (thumbs up/down) reweight subsequent queries. Coordinate-descent auto-tuner tunes alpha/beta knobs from accumulated feedback (v1.2.0 lessons).

Phased commits: each phase from the design doc gets one commit (sometimes more if cleanly separable). Conventional prefixes (feat:/fix:/chore:/docs:/test:/refactor:). HEREDOC for multi-line commit messages. Never skip phases or reorder.

Release branching policy: ONE branch per minor version (release/X.Y.0). Never chain X.Y+1 work onto release/X.Y.0. Branch off main, single chore(release) at tip, tag, PR, merge, then start next branch off refreshed main.

Pre-commit verification: full pytest suite + ruff check + ruff format. Even for docs-only commits. The README/docs link tests run against README content; a docs-only change can break CI on all 5 unit jobs (py3.11/3.12/3.13 + macOS + Windows).

Cytoscape minified core gotcha: 'evt.target === cy' fails on empty-canvas taps because the core is wrapped in an obfuscated class (e.g. Os). Use capability test 'typeof t.isNode !== function' instead.

Pulse animation saga: 3+ failed fixes on the same symptom means the architecture is wrong, not that the next amplitude tweak will land. Pulse rewrite from cy.animate to DOM overlay + CSS @keyframes worked first time after rejecting the cy.animate family.

Type-aware body preview: window.mnemoRenderBody(targetEl, body, {type, sourcePath}) is the single helper used by every preview surface (node detail Preview tab, search popover, future chat panel). Branches: code_* -> Prism, commit -> escaped pre, everything else -> marked markdown.
Daemon binds 127.0.0.1 only -- never 0.0.0.0. The whole product surface assumes local-only access; binding 0.0.0.0 would expose the API to the network and break the local-first security model.
No emojis in code, docs, or commit messages unless the user explicitly asks for them. Hard rule from CLAUDE.md, repo-wide. Linters don't catch this; reviewers must.
